data.title: British retailer Primark will get dressed up for its public trading debut next year
data.description:
Associated British Foods (ASBFY) (ASBFF) is planning to demerge Primark into a separately listed company by the end of 2027. Following the business split, the food, and agriculture businesses will remain under the Associated British Foods name. The company expects the split will improve long-term shareholder returns by giving both businesses a clearer investment case and more focused governance.

Under the plan, shareholders would own stakes in both businesses after the separation. Primark would become a stand-alone retail group, while the food business would keep the ABF name and include brands such as Twinings, Ryvita, Patak’s, grocery, ingredients, agriculture, and sugar. 

The rationale for the split is that the current conglomerate structure obscures the value of both businesses. Separating Primark should let investors value it more like a pure-play apparel retailer, while the food business can be viewed more cleanly as an FTSE 100 food producer. The board also said the demerger is the best way to maximize long-term returns for shareholders. Looking ahead, recurring dis-synergies of less than £45M are anticipated, and one-off separation and transaction costs of about £75M.

Primark competes with other fast-fashion and value apparel retailers such as Zara, H&M (HNNMY), Shein (SHEIN), Uniqlo (FRCOY) (FRCOF), Next, Matalan, New Look, Marks & Spencer (MAKSF) (MAKSY), ASOS (ASOMF) (ASOMY), River Island, and Mango.

data.title: Opacity in private credit can be an issue, says SEC Chair Paul Atkins
data.description:
Opacity in the private credit space can be an issue, and the SEC is monitoring emerging pressures within the sector, noted Paul Atkins, chairman of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Atkins took over as the SEC chief in April 2025. He was nominated by President Donald Trump, and is viewed as a pro-crypto, conservative, and business-friendly regulator.

"Advancing our regulatory posture also compels us to follow the capital flow as more of it finds its way into the private markets—a natural result of the heavy-handed regulation that forced banks to get out of the business of financing small and growing enterprises," he said.

"The SEC is closely monitoring both the lending gap that private credit has filled and the emerging pressures that it has experienced, including elevated redemption requests and rising default-rate projections," he said.

"Valuation, transparency, and credit quality are key," said the SEC chair. "Higher fees and less liquidity must be taken into account regarding the appropriateness of an investment." 

  • Private credit ETFs: (VPC), (HYIN), (PRIV), (PCMM), (PCLO), (PCR)

On digital assets, Paul Atkins said he called on the regulator to provide a firm regulatory foundation. "We are well into that process—and collaborating with our fellow regulators and Congress." 

"Innovation rarely pauses for regulation. And perhaps nowhere has the cost of failing to keep up been more apparent than in the agency's treatment of crypto assets," noted the SEC chair.

"An entire generation of digital asset innovation developed outside of the United States, not because American entrepreneurs lacked the ambition, or American investors lacked the appetite, but because American regulators lacked the will," he said.

"So, over the past year, this SEC has moved decisively on President Trump’s goal of making America the crypto capital of the world," said Atkins.

  • Bitcoin-backed ETFs: (IBIT), (ARKB), (GBTC), (BRRR), (BTCO), (HODL), (BTCW), (FBTC), (BITB), (EZBC)

According to the SEC chair, many rules that began as a framework to inform have become instruments to obscure over time.

"So, it should come as little surprise that as our disclosure burden expanded, the number of our public companies diminished," he said, noting that when he left the SEC as a staff member in 1994, more than 7,800 companies were listed on the U.S. exchanges, and when he returned as a chairman, that number fell by roughly 40%.

"We are working to reverse the precipitous decline in public companies. A central objective for this goal is to rationalize disclosure requirements by delivering the minimum dose of regulation," he said.

He said the near-term proposals in this direction include: adopting a regulatory IPO "on-ramp" that supplements the concept that Congress designed in the JOBS Act; expanding the existing accommodations that are currently available only for emerging and smaller companies to more businesses; providing nearly all public companies with an easier path to "shelf registration"; and giving companies the optionality for a quarterly or semiannual regulatory filing cadence.

  • IPO ETFs: (IPO), (IPOS), (FXPI)

data.title: Tump signals thaw with Anthropic despite Pentagon clash
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump said his administration expects to maintain a constructive relationship with Anthropic (ANTHRO), softening his tone toward the artificial intelligence firm despite its ongoing dispute with the Pentagon, Bloomberg reported.

Speaking in an interview with CNBC, as reported by Bloomberg, Trump said White House officials recently held “very good talks” with Anthropic executives and suggested tensions could ease. He described the company as politically aligned with the “radical left" but emphasized its technical capabilities and potential value to the government.

“They’re very smart… I think they can be of great use,” Trump said, adding that he expects the two sides to “get along just fine.”

In March, Anthropic (ANTHRO) sued the Trump administration after Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth labeled it a “supply-chain risk” in a dispute over a $200M contract with the Pentagon regarding the use of AI technology in classified systems.

The standoff has prompted the company to ramp up its Washington presence. Anthropic has hired Ballard Partners, a lobbying firm with close ties to the Trump administration, as it navigates the dispute.

Chief Executive Officer Dario Amodei also met senior White House officials last week, including Chief of Staff Susie Wiles, as the administration seeks broader access to the company’s advanced AI systems.

data.title: Churchill Downs to buy Preakness intellectual property rights for $85M
data.description:
• Churchill Downs (CHDN) on Tuesday said it has agreed to acquire the intellectual property rights for the Preakness Stakes and Black-Eyed Susan Stakes from 1/ST Maryland for $85 million.
  • The deal includes trademarks and associated rights tied to the races, with closing expected after the 2026 running of the Preakness Stakes, subject to customary conditions.
  • Under the agreement, Churchill Downs will license the intellectual property to the State of Maryland to allow the races to continue in exchange for an annual fee.
  • The Preakness Stakes, first run in 1873, is the second leg of U.S. thoroughbred racing’s Triple Crown, while the Black-Eyed Susan Stakes is a race for three-year-old fillies traditionally held the day before at Pimlico Race Course.
  • Churchill Downs said the transaction will be funded with cash on hand and its existing credit facility.
  • CHDN is +0.06% to $92.97
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Arista Networks sees bullish views at Susquehanna ahead of earnings
data.description:
Susquehanna raised the price target on Arista Networks' (ANET) stock to $200 from $160 ahead of the company's first quarter results on May 5.

The firm maintained its Positive rating on Arista's shares.

"We expect ANET to report in line with consensus but to guide above consensus for the June quarter,  as orders from its largest customer, Azure, are expected to strengthen. We also anticipate an upward revision to the 2026 revenue outlook to >25% growth, up from the current 25% guide.Consensus is already modeling ~27% growth. GM [gross margin] is also expected to trend toward 63-64%, or toward the high end of the 62-64% guidance range," said analysts led by Mehdi Hosseini.

The analysts added that Scale-Across opportunity remains underappreciated and is becoming an increasingly larger component of hyperscaler spending.

Scale-Across is a high-performance networking architecture aimed at connecting distributed AI training clusters across multiple data centers, campuses, or regions.

data.title: BOS Better Online Solutions secures $350,000 order extension
data.description:
• BOS Better Online Solutions (BOSC) announced on Tuesday that its RFID division received a $350,000 extension from an Israeli retail chain customer to a previous order of $540,000, which was reported on January 13, 2026. The extension brings the total value of this engagement to $890,000.
  • The customer operates a network of more than 300 stores across Israel, making it one of the country's largest retail chains, the company said. 
  • The company expects to deliver the extended order during the second quarter of 2026, following the original order, which was delivered during the first quarter of 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Webull authorizes $100M share buyback program
data.description:
• Webull (BULL) on Tuesday said its board approved a share repurchase program to buy back up to $100M of its Class A ordinary shares over the next 12 months.
  • The online brokerage said repurchases may be made from "time to time" through open market purchases, privately negotiated deals, block trades or other permitted methods, depending on market conditions.
  • Webull said it plans to fund the program using existing cash and future cash flow.
  • "It demonstrates our confidence in Webull's long-term outlook, financial position and growth prospects," CEO Anthony Denier said. 
  • "Share repurchase program reflects our balance sheet strength and our ability to return capital to shareholders while maintaining flexibility to continue investing in our growth priorities," said CFO H.C. Wang. 
  • The company said the buyback program may be changed, suspended or ended at any time.
  • Shares up 9% in premarket trading.

data.title: Stock market higher as hopes of peace deal between U.S. and Iran rise
data.description:
Wall Street rose on Tuesday as renewed hopes for a peace deal between Iran and the U.S. lifted sentiment.

The S&P 500 (SP500) +0.3%, the Dow (DJI) +0.4%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.1%.

Vice President JD Vance, along with the U.S. delegation, is expected to land in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Wednesday for another round of talks, CNN reported.

The Dow was further helped by better-than-expected results from UnitedHealth (UNH) in its Q1 2026 results. The company also increased its full-year earnings outlook.

U.S. Treasury yields edged higher across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.26%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) climbed 2 basis points to 3.75%. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) ticked up 0.5 basis points to 4.89%.

Retail sales jumped more than expected in March.

Investors were preparing for economic data releases, including business inventories and the pending home sales index.
